title: Ordered CatBoost Over Raw High-Cardinality Categories
summary: CatBoost’s ordered categorical handling and `has_time` mode are designed to avoid target leakage from random permutations when row order matters.
rationale: CatBoost’s ordered categorical handling and `has_time` mode are designed to avoid target leakage from random permutations when row order matters. This dataset has many rare `Driver`, `Race`, and interaction categories, so a CatBoost model can be complementary to the current manual-feature baseline even if feature importances overlap.
implementation_hint: Sort rows causally within `Year, Race, Driver, LapNumber`, set CatBoost `has_time=True`, feed raw categorical columns plus a small set of strongest numeric features, and validate with race-grouped folds only. Test interaction categories like `Driver+Compound` and `Race+Compound` as native categorical strings rather than manual frequency encodings.
expected_effect: May recover signal from sparse identities and interaction histories that one-hot/frequency/manual encodings miss, improving blend diversity more than standalone CV.
risk: If ordering is not truly causal or folds reuse the same race in train and validation, ordered CTRs can still look better locally than they generalize.
